decision.reason: The current dirty diff already contains focused low-level tests for both requested behaviors. Adding another test would duplicate coverage and increase conflict risk in an actively edited file.

materializable_entries_fix.behavior: Builds source-scoped local observation id sets once, then reuses them for relationEndpointIssues filtering instead of rebuilding the set by filtering accepted entries for each entry.

relation_target_local_id_backfill.behavior: Indexes local observation ids by resolved canonical record id and source, then backfills subject_local_observation_id/object_local_observation_id when relation payloads supply canonical subject_id/object_id directly.

timing_instrumentation.behavior: Adds opt-in MTA_MATERIALIZE_TIMING stderr timings without changing default output.

existing_focused_tests.name: does not materialize relations whose local endpoints exist only in another source
existing_focused_tests.covers: Same-source endpoint scoping is preserved by the precomputed local id index.

existing_focused_tests.name: keeps same-source relation endpoint filtering bounded for large accepted batches
existing_focused_tests.covers: O(N^2) materializableEntries regression by running 6000 accepted relation entries with missing same-source endpoints and asserting completion under 1000 ms with zero records.

existing_focused_tests.name: indexes repeated local observation ids without quadratic duplicate-set churn
existing_focused_tests.covers: O(N^2) duplicate local id set-churn regression by instrumenting Set iteration and ensuring repeated local ids do not repeatedly spread whole sets.

existing_focused_tests.name: backfills relation local endpoint ids when canonical record ids are supplied directly
existing_focused_tests.covers: Relation payloads with canonical subject_id/object_id get source-local endpoint ids filled.

existing_focused_tests.name: prefers same-source local endpoint ids when backfilling merged canonical relation targets
existing_focused_tests.covers: Backfill chooses the relation source's local ids for merged canonical targets, falling back only when same-source local ids are unavailable.

patch_plan_if_these_tests_were_absent.change: Add a same-source endpoint filtering regression test with thousands of accepted relation entries whose endpoints are missing in the same source; assert entriesToRecords returns no records and stays under a conservative local threshold.

Add a direct canonical relation endpoint test where project/entity records exist and the relation supplies subject_id/object_id only; assert the materialized relation retains canonical ids and backfills subject_local_observation_id/object_local_observation_id.
Add a merged-target variant where source A and source B local ids resolve to the same canonical project/entity, then assert a source B relation gets source B local ids.

residual_risk: The 1000 ms performance assertion is intentionally coarse and environment-sensitive; it catches catastrophic O(N^2) regressions but is not a benchmark.
The Set iterator instrumentation catches duplicate local id churn in localBuckets/uniqueLocal construction, but does not measure all possible Map/Set allocations.
The tests cover entriesToRecords behavior directly and do not run full materialization, which was intentionally avoided by assignment.

command_results.bun_test_packages_pipeline_test_material: pass: 18 tests, 0 fail, 692 expect() calls, 703 ms
command_results.git_diff_stat_inspected: packages/pipeline/src/materialize/materialize.ts plus focused materialize/relations tests were already modified in the dirty worktree before this note
